channel.title: ChromeOS Project — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by ChromeOS Project (chromeos).

item.title: CVE-2025-6044 · 6.1 MEDIUM
item.description: An Improper Access Control vulnerability in the Stylus Tools component of Google ChromeOS version 16238.64.0 on the garaged stylus devices allows a physical attacker to bypass the lock screen and access user files by removing the stylus while the dev…

item.title: CVE-2025-6179 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — ChromeOS Extension Disablement and Developer Mode Bypass via ExtHang3r and ExtPrint3r Exploits
item.description: Permissions Bypass in Extension Management in Google ChromeOS 16181.27.0 on managed Chrome devices allows a local attacker to disable extensions and access Developer Mode, including loading additional extensions via exploiting vulnerabilities using t…

item.title: CVE-2025-6177 · 7.4 HIGH — ChromeOS MiniOS Root Code Execution Bypass While Dev Mode Blocked
item.description: Privilege Escalation in MiniOS in Google ChromeOS (16063.45.2 and potentially others) on enrolled devices allows a local attacker to gain root code execution via exploiting a debug shell (VT3 console) accessible through specific key combinations duri…

item.title: CVE-2025-2509 · 7.8 HIGH
item.description: Out-of-Bounds Read in Virglrenderer in ChromeOS 16093.57.0 allows a malicious guest VM to achieve arbitrary address access within the crosvm sandboxed process, potentially leading to VM escape via crafted vertex elements data triggering an out-of-bou…

item.title: CVE-2025-1290 · 8.1 HIGH 🌩️
item.description: A race condition Use-After-Free vulnerability exists in the virtio_transport_space_update function within the Kernel 5.4 on ChromeOS. Concurrent allocation and freeing of the virtio_vsock_sock structure during an AF_VSOCK connect syscall can occur be…

item.title: CVE-2025-1568 · 8.8 HIGH
item.description: Access Control Vulnerability in Gerrit chromiumos project configuration in Google ChromeOS 16063.87.0 allows an attacker with a registered Gerrit account to inject malicious code into ChromeOS projects and potentially achieve Remote Code Execution an…

item.title: CVE-2025-2073 · 8.8 HIGH
item.description: Out-of-Bounds Read in netfilter/ipset in Linux Kernel ChromeOS [6.1, 5.15, 5.10, 5.4, 4.19] allows a local attacker with low privileges to trigger an out-of-bounds read, potentially leading to information disclosure

item.title: CVE-2025-1704 · 6.5 MEDIUM
item.description: ComponentInstaller Modification in ComponentInstaller in Google ChromeOS 15823.23.0 on Chromebooks allows enrolled users with local access to unenroll devices and intercept device management requests via loading components from the unencrypted statef…

item.title: CVE-2025-1566 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️
item.description: DNS Leak in Native System VPN in Google ChromeOS Dev Channel on ChromeOS 16002.23.0 allows network observers to expose plaintext DNS queries via failure to properly tunnel DNS traffic during VPN state transitions.

item.title: CVE-2025-1122 · 6.7 MEDIUM
item.description: Out-Of-Bounds Write in TPM2 Reference Library in Google ChromeOS 15753.50.0 stable on Cr50 Boards allows an attacker with root access to gain persistence and Bypass operating system verification via exploiting the NV_Read functionality during the Cha…

item.title: CVE-2025-1292 · 6.7 MEDIUM — TPM2 Out-Of-Bounds Write Leading to Potential Operating System Verification Bypass in ChromeOS
item.description: Out-Of-Bounds Write in TPM2 Reference Library in Google ChromeOS 122.0.6261.132 stable on Cr50 Boards allows an attacker with root access to gain persistence and bypass operating system verification via exploiting the NV_Read functionality during the…

item.title: CVE-2025-1121 · 6.8 MEDIUM
item.description: Privilege escalation in Installer and Recovery image handling in Google ChromeOS version 15786.48.2 on device allows an attacker with physical access to gain root code execution and potentially unenroll enterprise-managed devices via a specially craf…
